Title: Linalool and 1,8‐Cineole as Constitutive Disease‐Resistant Factors of Norway Spruce Against Necrotrophic Pathogen Heterobasidion parviporum

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate-derived monoterpene precursor to form 1,8-cineole
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Constitutive antifungal defense against the necrotrophic pathogen Heterobasidion parviporum; associated with disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Needles and young stem/branches adjacent to infection site; monoterpenes are also discussed as present in multiple spruce tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with higher 1,8-cineole accumulation and partial resistance to Heterobasidion parviporum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transcript(s) annotated as 1,8-cineole synthase were upregulated in partial resistant clones. 2. Partial resistant clones accumulated significantly more 1,8-cineole than susceptible clones. 3. 1,8-cineole inhibited Heterobasidion parviporum growth and distorted hyphal morphology, supporting its role as a constitutive resistance factor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq between partial resistant and susceptible clones; terpene metabolite profiling by GC-MS; in vitro antifungal assay with 1,8-cineole; pathogen morphology observation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in needles/branches of partial resistant clones before inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in needles/branches of partial resistant clones before inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive pre-infection state; comparison of partial resistant versus susceptible clones; pathogen challenge context with Heterobasidion parviporum in resistance screening
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Norway spruce clone needles; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate marker and engineering target for breeding Norway spruce with constitutive resistance; may be prioritized for selection or stacked with linalool synthase/high jasmonate- and phenylpropanoid-associated traits
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant correlation between lesion development and tree height increase was observed during the infection period; other tradeoffs not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats this as a core candidate determinant of resistance, but no single-gene transgenic or biochemical purification validation was reported.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geranyl diphosphate-derived monoterpene precursor to linalool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Constitutive antifungal defense against the necrotrophic pathogen Heterobasidion parviporum; associated with disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Needles and young stem/branches adjacent to infection site
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with higher linalool accumulation and partial resistance to Heterobasidion parviporum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transcript(s) annotated as possible linalool synthase genes were upregulated in partial resistant clones. 2. Partial resistant clones accumulated significantly more linalool than susceptible clones. 3. Linalool inhibited Heterobasidion parviporum growth and caused abnormal hyphal morphology. 4. Combined linalool plus 1,8-cineole treatment showed stronger inhibition than either alone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq between partial resistant and susceptible clones; terpene metabolite profiling by GC-MS; in vitro antifungal assay with linalool and mixed terpene treatment; pathogen morphology observation; pathogen RNA-seq after linalool/mixed treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in needles/branches of partial resistant clones before inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in needles/branches of partial resistant clones before inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive pre-infection state; comparison of partial resistant versus susceptible clones; pathogen challenge context with Heterobasidion parviporum
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Norway spruce clone needles and RNA-seq of terpene-treated Heterobasidion parviporum; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value marker and engineering target for constitutive disease resistance breeding; likely best deployed together with 1,8-cineole synthase and other favorable defense-metabolism traits
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant correlation between lesion development and tree height increase was observed during the infection period; additional tradeoffs not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated as 'possible' linalool synthase genes in the paper, indicating catalytic assignment was inferred from annotation/expression rather than direct enzyme assay.
